distractor_explanations.に: に marks time / destination / recipient — not the role required here.
distractor_explanations.が: が marks the grammatical subject (esp. with stative verbs / desire) — not what fits here.

distractor_explanations_hi.の: の संज्ञा-जोड़क (सम्बन्ध) या नामकरण — यहाँ नहीं फिट।
distractor_explanations_hi.に: に समय / गंतव्य / पात्र चिह्नक — यहाँ अपेक्षित भूमिका नहीं।
distractor_explanations_hi.が: が व्याकरणिक कर्ता चिह्नक (स्थिति-क्रिया / इच्छा के साथ) — यहाँ नहीं फिट।

questions.prompt_ja: （ ）に いちばん いい ものを えらんで ください。
questions.question_ja: すみません、おちゃ（ ）おねがいします。
questions.explanation_en: Noun + を + onegaishimasu = I'd like [noun] please (more polite than wo kudasai). Same を-marks-the-thing pattern. Distractors: に/で/と don't fit this request frame.
questions.explanation_hi: संज्ञा + を + おねがいします = "मुझे [संज्ञा] चाहिए" (くださいसे विनम्र)।

distractor_explanations.に: に marks time / destination / recipient — not the role required here.
distractor_explanations.で: で marks location of action or means / instrument — not the role required here.
distractor_explanations.と: と means 'with / and (exhaustive)' — not what fits here.

distractor_explanations_hi.に: に समय / गंतव्य / पात्र चिह्नक — यहाँ अपेक्षित भूमिका नहीं।
distractor_explanations_hi.で: で क्रिया-स्थान या साधन/उपकरण चिह्नक — यहाँ अपेक्षित भूमिका नहीं।
distractor_explanations_hi.と: と 'के साथ / और (पूर्ण-गणना)' — यहाँ नहीं फिट।

questions.prompt_ja: （ ）に いちばん いい ものを えらんで ください。
questions.question_ja: コーヒー（ ）いかがですか。
questions.explanation_en: Noun は ikaga desu ka = how about [noun]? (polite offer). は marks the noun as topic of the offer. Distractors: を/に/が don't fit the offer-introduction pattern.
questions.explanation_hi: संज्ञा + は + いかがですか = "[संज्ञा] कैसा रहेगा?" (विनम्र प्रस्ताव)।

distractor_explanations.を: を marks the direct object — not the role required here.
distractor_explanations.に: に marks time / destination / recipient — not the role required here.
distractor_explanations.が: が marks the grammatical subject (esp. with stative verbs / desire) — not what fits here.

distractor_explanations_hi.を: を प्रत्यक्ष कर्म चिह्नक — यहाँ अपेक्षित भूमिका नहीं।
distractor_explanations_hi.に: に समय / गंतव्य / पात्र चिह्नक — यहाँ अपेक्षित भूमिका नहीं।
distractor_explanations_hi.が: が व्याकरणिक कर्ता चिह्नक (स्थिति-क्रिया / इच्छा के साथ) — यहाँ नहीं फिट।

questions.prompt_ja: （ ）に いちばん いい ものを えらんで ください。
questions.question_ja: 「ごはんを たべましたか。」「いいえ、（ ）たべて いません。」
questions.explanation_en: mada + V-te imasen = not yet (action still pending). The negative answer 'no, I have not eaten yet' pins まだ. Distractors: もう would pair with the affirmative 'already ate'; いま (now) and すぐ (right away) don't fit the not-yet frame.
questions.explanation_hi: まだ + V-て いません = "अभी नहीं" (कार्य लंबित)।

distractor_explanations.いま: いま means 'now' — not what fits here.
distractor_explanations.もう: もう means 'already' — not what fits here.
distractor_explanations.すぐ: すぐ means 'right away / immediately' — not what fits here.

distractor_explanations_hi.もう: もう 'पहले से' — यहाँ नहीं फिट।

questions.prompt_ja: （ ）に いちばん いい ものを えらんで ください。
questions.question_ja: 「しゅくだいを しましたか。」「はい、（ ）しました。」
questions.explanation_en: mou + V-mashita = already (action completed). The affirmative answer 'yes, already did' pins もう. Distractors: まだ would pair with negative; いま (now) doesn't match completed-past; あとで (later) is future.
questions.explanation_hi: もう + V-ました = "पहले से ही" (कार्य पूर्ण)।

distractor_explanations.あとで: あとで means 'later (afterward)' — not what fits here.
distractor_explanations.まだ: まだ means 'still / not yet' — not what fits here.
distractor_explanations.いま: いま means 'now' — not what fits here.

distractor_explanations_hi.あとで: あとで 'बाद में' — यहाँ नहीं फिट।
distractor_explanations_hi.まだ: まだ 'अभी भी / अभी नहीं' — यहाँ नहीं फिट।

questions.prompt_ja: （ ）に いちばん いい ものを えらんで ください。
questions.question_ja: この りょうりは おいしいです（ ）、すこし からいです。
questions.explanation_en: Mid-sentence が = but (contrast connector). 'Tasty BUT a bit spicy.' Distractors: から/ので are causal (because); まで is endpoint.
questions.explanation_hi: मध्य-वाक्य が = "लेकिन"। "स्वादिष्ट लेकिन थोड़ा तीखा"।

distractor_explanations.から: から means 'from (origin)' or 'because' — not what fits here.
distractor_explanations.ので: ので means 'because (soft, factual)' — not what fits here.
distractor_explanations.まで: まで means 'until / up to (endpoint)' — not what fits here.

distractor_explanations_hi.から: から 'से (मूल)' या 'क्योंकि' — यहाँ नहीं फिट।
distractor_explanations_hi.ので: ので 'क्योंकि (कोमल, तथ्यात्मक)' — यहाँ नहीं फिट।

questions.prompt_ja: （ ）に いちばん いい ものを えらんで ください。
questions.question_ja: あした、たぶん あめが ふる（ ）。
questions.explanation_en: deshou = probably / I expect (paired with tabun 'probably' in the stem for emphasis). Distractors: deshita (past copula), desu ka (question ending -  would change meaning), deshite (て-form, ungrammatical sentence-finally).
questions.explanation_hi: でしょう = "शायद/अपेक्षित"। たぶん के साथ अनुमान पर ज़ोर।

distractor_explanations.でした: でした is the polite past copula — not what fits here.
distractor_explanations.ですか: ですか turns the copula sentence into a question — not what's required here.
distractor_explanations.でして: でして is a stiff/non-standard copula form — not what fits here.

distractor_explanations_hi.でした: でした विनम्र भूत कोपुला — यहाँ नहीं फिट।
distractor_explanations_hi.ですか: ですか कोपुला-वाक्य को प्रश्न में बदलता है — यहाँ अपेक्षित नहीं।
distractor_explanations_hi.でして: でして अनौपचारिक/असामान्य कोपुला रूप — यहाँ नहीं फिट।

questions.prompt_ja: （ ）に いちばん いい ものを えらんで ください。
questions.question_ja: あの ひとは たなか（ ）です。
questions.explanation_en: san is the standard polite name suffix (Mr./Ms.) for adults in neutral contexts. Distractors: chan (young children / very close friends), kun (young men / juniors), sama (extremely formal -  overkill for everyday use).
questions.explanation_hi: さん वयस्कों के लिए मानक विनम्र नाम-प्रत्यय। ちゃん बच्चों के लिए, くん युवा पुरुषों के लिए।

distractor_explanations.くん: くん is a masculine honorific suffix (mostly for boys / juniors) — not what fits here.
distractor_explanations.ちゃん: ちゃん is an intimate honorific suffix (children / close friends) — not what fits here.
distractor_explanations.さま: さま is the highest honorific suffix (customers / deities) — not what fits here.

distractor_explanations_hi.くん: くん पुल्लिंग सम्मानार्थक प्रत्यय (लड़कों/जूनियर के लिए) — यहाँ नहीं फिट।
distractor_explanations_hi.ちゃん: ちゃん अंतरंग सम्मानार्थक प्रत्यय (बच्चों/घनिष्ठ मित्रों के लिए) — यहाँ नहीं फिट।
distractor_explanations_hi.さま: さま उच्चतम सम्मानार्थक प्रत्यय (ग्राहक/देवता) — यहाँ नहीं फिट।

questions.prompt_ja: （ ）に いちばん いい ものを えらんで ください。
questions.question_ja: （ ）ちゃを のみますか。
questions.explanation_en: o-cha = (honorific) tea. Native-Japanese-origin words usually take お (o-); Sino-Japanese words take ご (go-). Distractors: ご is for Sino-Japanese stems (gohan, gokazoku); の is possessive; を is object marker.
questions.explanation_hi: お-चा = (आदर) चाय। मूल जापानी शब्द आमतौर पर お लेते हैं; चीनी-मूल ご लेते हैं।

distractor_explanations.を: を marks the direct object — not the role required here.
distractor_explanations.ご: ご is an honorific prefix (for Sino-Japanese nouns) — not what fits here.
distractor_explanations.の: の links nouns (possessive / attributive) or nominalizes — not what fits here.

distractor_explanations_hi.を: を प्रत्यक्ष कर्म चिह्नक — यहाँ अपेक्षित भूमिका नहीं।
distractor_explanations_hi.ご: ご सम्मानार्थक उपसर्ग (चीन-जापानी संज्ञाओं के लिए) — यहाँ नहीं फिट।
distractor_explanations_hi.の: の संज्ञा-जोड़क (सम्बन्ध) या नामकरण — यहाँ नहीं फिट।

questions.prompt_ja: （ ）に いちばん いい ものを えらんで ください。
questions.question_ja: にちようびは ほんを よんだり、おんがくを きいたり（ ）。
questions.explanation_en: V-tari V-tari + suru = do A and B (among other things). The pattern needs the suru helper at the end to bind the listed activities. Distractors: arimasu/imasu are existence verbs; narimasu is 'become' -  none bind tari-listings.
questions.explanation_hi: V-たり V-たり + する = "X और Y (आदि) करना"। する सहायक अंत में अनिवार्य।

distractor_explanations.あります: あります is the verb 'to exist (inanimate)' — not what fits here.
distractor_explanations.います: います is the verb 'to exist (animate)' — not what fits here.
distractor_explanations.なります: なります is 'to become' polite non-past — not what fits here.

distractor_explanations_hi.あります: あります 'होना (निर्जीव)' क्रिया — यहाँ नहीं फिट।
distractor_explanations_hi.います: います 'होना (सजीव)' क्रिया — यहाँ नहीं फिट।
distractor_explanations_hi.なります: なります 'बनना' विनम्र वर्तमान — यहाँ नहीं फिट।

questions.prompt_ja: （ ）に いちばん いい ものを えらんで ください。
questions.question_ja: わたしは にほんへ 行った（ ）が あります。
questions.explanation_en: V-ta + koto + ga arimasu = have done [V] before (experiential). Only こと forms this experience-pattern. Distractors: もの is a physical thing; ところ is a place; とき is a time/occasion.
questions.explanation_hi: V-た + こと + が あります = "पहले X किया है" (अनुभव)। केवल こと।

distractor_explanations.もの: もの means 'thing (concrete)' — not what fits here.
distractor_explanations.ところ: ところ means 'place' or 'moment' — not what fits here.
distractor_explanations.とき: とき means 'when / at the time of' — not what fits here.

distractor_explanations_hi.ところ: ところ 'जगह' या 'क्षण' — यहाँ नहीं फिट।
distractor_explanations_hi.とき: とき 'जब / के समय' — यहाँ नहीं फिट।

questions.prompt_ja: （ ）に いちばん いい ものを えらんで ください。
questions.question_ja: つかれた ときは、はやく ねた（ ）が いいです。
questions.explanation_en: V-ta + hou + ga + ii = should do [V] (recommendation). Only ほう forms this recommendation pattern. Distractors: もの/こと/ところ don't fit this comparative-recommendation frame.
questions.explanation_hi: V-た + ほう + が + いい = "X करना चाहिए"। केवल ほう।

distractor_explanations.こと: こと means 'thing (abstract / matter)' — not what fits here.
distractor_explanations.もの: もの means 'thing (concrete)' — not what fits here.
distractor_explanations.ところ: ところ means 'place' or 'moment' — not what fits here.

distractor_explanations_hi.ところ: ところ 'जगह' या 'क्षण' — यहाँ नहीं फिट।

questions.prompt_ja: （ ）に いちばん いい ものを えらんで ください。
questions.question_ja: この にもつは おも（ ）。
questions.explanation_en: い-adj-stem (drop い) + sugimasu = too [adj]. omoi → omo + sugimasu = 'too heavy'. Distractors: sugiru (plain form, would need different polite-level context), sugite (て-form, conjunction not a sentence ender), suginai (negative -  would mean 'not too heavy' which contradicts the implied complaint).
questions.explanation_hi: い-विशेषण-तना (い हटाओ) + すぎます = "बहुत [विशेषण]"। おもい → おもすぎます।

distractor_explanations.すぎない: すぎない is plain negative of 'excessive' — not what fits here.
distractor_explanations.すぎる: すぎる means 'too much / excessive' — not what fits here.
distractor_explanations.すぎて: すぎて is te-form of 'excessive' — not what fits here.

distractor_explanations_hi.すぎない: すぎない 'अति' का सादा नकार — यहाँ नहीं फिट।
distractor_explanations_hi.すぎる: すぎる 'बहुत अधिक / अति' — यहाँ नहीं फिट।
distractor_explanations_hi.すぎて: すぎて 'अति' का -te रूप — यहाँ नहीं फिट।

questions.prompt_ja: （ ）に いちばん いい ものを えらんで ください。
questions.question_ja: らいねん、にほんへ 行く（ ）です。
questions.explanation_en: V-plain + tsumori desu = intend to do [V]. Only つもり forms this intention pattern. Distractors: もの (physical thing), こと (matter/fact), とき (time/occasion) don't carry the future-intention sense.
questions.explanation_hi: V-सादा + つもり です = "X करने का इरादा"। केवल つもり।

distractor_explanations.もの: もの means 'thing (concrete)' — not what fits here.
distractor_explanations.こと: こと means 'thing (abstract / matter)' — not what fits here.
distractor_explanations.とき: とき means 'when / at the time of' — not what fits here.

distractor_explanations_hi.とき: とき 'जब / के समय' — यहाँ नहीं फिट।

questions.prompt_ja: （ ）に いちばん いい ものを えらんで ください。
questions.question_ja: （みせの 中で）すみません、トイレは（ ）ですか。
questions.explanation_en: Asking for the location of something requires the question demonstrative どこ (where). Distractors: ここ/そこ are statements (here/there) -  declarative, not interrogative; なに asks WHAT, not WHERE.
questions.explanation_hi: किसी चीज़ का स्थान पूछना = どこ (कहाँ?)।

distractor_explanations.ここ: ここ is 'here (place near speaker)' — not what fits here.
distractor_explanations.そこ: そこ is 'there (place near listener)' — not what fits here.
distractor_explanations.なに: なに is 'what?' — not what fits here.

distractor_explanations_hi.そこ: そこ 'वहाँ (सुनने वाले के पास)' — यहाँ नहीं फिट।

questions.prompt_ja: （ ）に いちばん いい ものを えらんで ください。
questions.question_ja: クラスで（ ）せが たかい ひとは たなかさんです。
questions.explanation_en: いちばん = #1 / most / -est. Combined with an い-adjective it forms the superlative. Distractors: とても (very) is degree, not superlative; もっと (more) is comparative, not superlative; あまり needs a negative.
questions.explanation_hi: いちばん = "सर्वोच्च/सबसे"। い-विशेषण के साथ श्रेष्ठतम बनाता है।

distractor_explanations.もっと: もっと means 'more' (comparative) — not what fits here.
distractor_explanations.とても: とても means 'very' — not what fits here.
distractor_explanations.あまり: あまり means 'not very (with negative)' — not what fits here.

distractor_explanations_hi.あまり: あまり 'बहुत (नकारात्मक के साथ)' — यहाँ नहीं फिट।

questions.prompt_ja: （ ）に いちばん いい ものを えらんで ください。
questions.question_ja: わたしは 七時（ ）あさごはんを たべます。
questions.explanation_en: Specific clock-times take に. Distractors: で is means/place-of-action (七時で = ungrammatical for times); へ is destination direction (times don't take direction); を is object marker.
questions.explanation_hi: विशिष्ट घड़ी-समय に लेते हैं।

distractor_explanations.を: を marks the direct object — not the role required here.
distractor_explanations.で: で marks location of action or means / instrument — not the role required here.
distractor_explanations.へ: へ marks direction toward — not what fits here.

distractor_explanations_hi.を: を प्रत्यक्ष कर्म चिह्नक — यहाँ अपेक्षित भूमिका नहीं।
distractor_explanations_hi.で: で क्रिया-स्थान या साधन/उपकरण चिह्नक — यहाँ अपेक्षित भूमिका नहीं।
distractor_explanations_hi.へ: へ की-ओर दिशा-चिह्नक — यहाँ नहीं फिट।

questions.prompt_ja: （ ）に いちばん いい ものを えらんで ください。
questions.question_ja: あめが ふっています（ ）、いえに います。
questions.explanation_en: Sentence + から = because + result. Rain → so I'm staying home. Distractors: けれど is contrast (but); まで is endpoint; より is comparison.
questions.explanation_hi: वाक्य + から = "क्योंकि" + परिणाम।

distractor_explanations.けれど: けれど means 'but / however' — not what fits here.
distractor_explanations.まで: まで means 'until / up to (endpoint)' — not what fits here.
distractor_explanations.より: より means 'than (comparison)' — not what fits here.

distractor_explanations_hi.けれど: けれど 'पर / हालाँकि' — यहाँ नहीं फिट।

questions.prompt_ja: （ ）に いちばん いい ものを えらんで ください。
questions.question_ja: わたしは 毎日 ８じに（ ）おきます。
questions.explanation_en: 毎日 (every day) anchors a habitual action; いつも (always) is the matching frequency adverb. Distractors: あまり and ぜんぜん require a negative verb (ません/ない); ときどき (sometimes) contradicts 毎日.
questions.explanation_hi: 毎日 (हर दिन) आदतन कार्य; いつも (हमेशा) मेल खाने वाला आवृत्ति-क्रिया-विशेषण।

distractor_explanations.あまり: あまり means 'not very (with negative)' — not what fits here.
distractor_explanations.ぜんぜん: ぜんぜん means 'not at all (with negative)' — not what fits here.
distractor_explanations.ときどき: ときどき means 'sometimes' — not what fits here.

distractor_explanations_hi.あまり: あまり 'बहुत (नकारात्मक के साथ)' — यहाँ नहीं फिट।
distractor_explanations_hi.ぜんぜん: ぜんぜん 'बिल्कुल नहीं (नकारात्मक के साथ)' — यहाँ नहीं फिट।
distractor_explanations_hi.ときどき: ときどき 'कभी-कभी' — यहाँ नहीं फिट।

questions.prompt_ja: （ ）に いちばん いい ものを えらんで ください。
questions.question_ja: （ ）すわって ください。
questions.explanation_en: どうぞ + V-て ください = please go ahead and [V] / please [V] (offering / inviting). Distractors: どうも is gratitude (thanks), すみません is apology, おねがい is requesting a favor (won't pair with V-て + ください).
questions.explanation_hi: どうぞ + V-て ください = "कृपया ~ करें" (आमंत्रण/प्रस्ताव)।

distractor_explanations.すみません: すみません is 'excuse me / sorry / thank you (apologetic)' — not what fits here.
distractor_explanations.どうも: どうも is 'thanks (casual) / indeed' — not what fits here.
distractor_explanations.おねがい: おねがい means 'please / request' — not what fits here.

distractor_explanations_hi.すみません: すみません 'क्षमा करें / माफ़ी / धन्यवाद' — यहाँ नहीं फिट।
distractor_explanations_hi.どうも: どうも 'धन्यवाद (अनौपचारिक) / वास्तव में' — यहाँ नहीं फिट।
distractor_explanations_hi.おねがい: おねがい 'कृपया / अनुरोध' — यहाँ नहीं फिट।

questions.prompt_ja: （ ）に いちばん いい ものを えらんで ください。
questions.question_ja:
A: あついです（ ）。
B: ええ、ほんとうに あついですね。
questions.explanation_en: ね seeks confirmation/agreement; B's response 'ええ、ほんとうに...ですね' (yes, really hot) confirms agreement, which only pairs with ね from A. Distractors: よ is assertive (informing -  B wouldn't agree-back as enthusiastically), か would make A's line a question, の is informal-question.
questions.explanation_hi: ね सहमति/पुष्टि माँगता है; B का "हाँ, सच में..." जवाब केवल ね से जुड़ता है।

distractor_explanations.の: の links nouns (possessive / attributive) or nominalizes — not what fits here.

questions.explanation_en: Plain-form + だろう = probably / I expect (casual variant of でしょう). たぶん (probably) anchors the speculation. Distractors: だった is plain past, だ is plain affirmative (no speculation), じゃない is negation.

questions.explanation_en: ね seeks confirmation; B's そうですね confirms agreement, which uniquely pairs with ね. Distractors: よ would assert; か would query; の is informal.
questions.explanation_hi: ね पुष्टि माँगता है; そうですね सहमति देता है — विशिष्ट रूप से ね से जुड़ता है।

questions.question_ja: じゅぎょう（ ）あとで、ともだちと あいます。
questions.explanation_en: Noun の あとで = after [Noun]. The の is the genitive linking noun → temporal-noun (あと). Distractors: を/に/が don't link nouns to あと in this temporal pattern.
questions.explanation_hi: संज्ञा の あとで = "[संज्ञा] के बाद"।

questions.prompt_ja: （ ）に いちばん いい ものを えらんで ください。
questions.question_ja: ごはん（ ）まえに、てを あらいます。
questions.explanation_en: Noun の まえに = before [Noun]. Same genitive-link pattern as n5-160 with まえ. Distractors: を/に/で don't link nouns to まえ in this temporal pattern.
questions.explanation_hi: संज्ञा の まえに = "[संज्ञा] से पहले"।

questions.prompt_ja: （ ）に いちばん いい ものを えらんで ください。
questions.question_ja: ねる（ ）に、はを みがきます。
questions.explanation_en: V-plain (non-past) + まえに = before [V]-ing. Sequence: brush teeth → go to bed. Distractors: あと would need V-past form (ねた あとで); とき is when; うち is while/within.
questions.explanation_hi: V-सादा (वर्तमान) + まえに = "करने से पहले"। दाँत साफ़ करना → सोना।

distractor_explanations.あと: あと means 'after' (typically with verb-た) — not what fits here.

distractor_explanations_hi.あと: あと 'के बाद' (आमतौर पर क्रिया-た के साथ) — यहाँ नहीं फिट।
distractor_explanations_hi.とき: とき 'जब / के समय' — यहाँ नहीं फिट।
distractor_explanations_hi.うち: うち 'घर / अंदर' — यहाँ नहीं फिट।

questions.prompt_ja: （ ）に いちばん いい ものを えらんで ください。

questions.explanation_en: V-past (た-form) + あとで = after [V]-ing. Sequence: ate → then homework. Distractors: まえ would need non-past (たべる まえに); とき is when (different aspect); ところ is moment-of-action.
questions.explanation_hi: V-भूत (た-रूप) + あとで = "करने के बाद"। खा लिया → फिर होमवर्क।

distractor_explanations.まえ: まえ means 'before' (typically with verb-dictionary form) — not what fits here.

distractor_explanations_hi.ところ: ところ 'जगह' या 'क्षण' — यहाँ नहीं फिट।

questions.prompt_ja: （ ）に いちばん いい ものを えらんで ください。
questions.question_ja: ごはんを たべる まえに、（ ）と 言います。
questions.explanation_en: いただきます is said BEFORE eating (literally 'I humbly receive'). Distractors: ごちそうさま is said AFTER eating; おはようございます is morning greeting; おやすみなさい is good-night.
questions.explanation_hi: いただきます खाने से पहले कहा जाता है। ごちそうさま बाद में।

distractor_explanations.おはようございます: おはようございます is 'good morning' — not the greeting fitting here.
distractor_explanations.ごちそうさま: ごちそうさま is said AFTER eating — not the greeting fitting here.
distractor_explanations.おやすみなさい: おやすみなさい is 'good night' (before sleep) — not the greeting fitting here.

distractor_explanations_hi.ごちそうさま: ごちそうさま खाने के बाद कहा जाता है — यहाँ उपयुक्त अभिवादन नहीं।

questions.prompt_ja: （ ）に いちばん いい ものを えらんで ください。
questions.question_ja:

questions.explanation_en: Plain + んです = explanation form (giving a reason for a state/situation). A asks 'why', B explains with んです. Distractors: ます is non-past polite (wrong tense -  past pain), でした copula (would need adjective ending い→かった directly + でした), ました past polite verb ending (the predicate is an い-adj, not a verb).
questions.explanation_hi: सादा + んです = व्याख्या-रूप (कारण/स्थिति देता है)।

distractor_explanations.ました: ました is the polite past verb ending — not the form required here.

questions.question_ja: あぶないですから、そこへ 行か（ ）ほうが いいです。
questions.explanation_en: V-ない + ほうが いい = shouldn't [V] (negative recommendation). The slot needs the negative-plain form ない. Distractors: ます is polite-affirmative; た is past affirmative; て is conjunction form -  none combine with ほうが いい to form 'shouldn't'.
questions.explanation_hi: V-ない + ほうが いい = "नहीं करना चाहिए"। नकारात्मक सिफ़ारिश।

questions.explanation_hi: V-तना + なくても いい = "करने की ज़रूरत नहीं"।

questions.explanation_en: V-stem + なくては + いけない = must [V] (obligation). The いけません ending forces なくては. Distractors: なくても goes with いい (don't have to); ないで is V-without-doing; なくて is conjunction without obligation.
questions.explanation_hi: V-तना + なくては + いけない = "अवश्य करना होगा" (दायित्व)।

questions.explanation_en: V-stem + なくては + ならない = must [V] (formal obligation, same as いけない). The なりません ending forces なくては. Distractors: なくても is permission-not-to (with いい); ないで is V-without-doing; なくて is bare conjunction.
questions.explanation_hi: V-तना + なくては + ならない = "अवश्य करना होगा" (औपचारिक)।

questions.explanation_en: V-ない + と + いけない = must [V] (common spoken variant of must). The いけません ending forces ないと. Distractors: なくて is bare conjunction; ないで is V-without-doing; なくちゃ doesn't pair with いけません (it's already a contraction containing いけない).
questions.explanation_hi: V-ない + と + いけない = "अवश्य करना होगा" (बोलचाल का सामान्य रूप)।

distractor_explanations.なくて: なくて is the negative te-form used for 'reason / cause' — not what fits here.

distractor_explanations.なくちゃ: なくちゃ is casual 'must (do)' — not the register / form fitting here.

questions.explanation_hi: V-तना + なくちゃ = "करना ज़रूरी है" (अनौपचारिक संक्षिप्त)।

questions.explanation_hi: सादा + って + 言う = अनौपचारिक उद्धरण-सूचक (と का बोलचाल समकक्ष)।

questions.prompt_ja: （ ）に いちばん いい ものを えらんで ください。

questions.explanation_hi: क्रिया-तना (ます हटाओ) + かた = "करने का तरीक़ा"। 食べかた = "खाने का तरीक़ा"।

distractor_explanations.ひと: ひと means 'person' — not what fits here.
distractor_explanations.とき: とき means 'when / at the time of' — not what fits here.
distractor_explanations.ばしょ: ばしょ means 'place / location' — not what fits here.

distractor_explanations_hi.とき: とき 'जब / के समय' — यहाँ नहीं फिट।
distractor_explanations_hi.ばしょ: ばしょ 'जगह / स्थान' — यहाँ नहीं फिट।

questions.prompt_ja: （ ）に いちばん いい ものを えらんで ください。
questions.question_ja: 「うわあ、この けしきは きれいだ（ ）。」
questions.explanation_en: なあ is a sentence-final exclamation expressing strong personal feeling ('really, isn't it?'). The 「うわあ」 opening interjection anchors strong emotional register, which only なあ matches. Distractors: ね seeks confirmation (less self-directed than なあ), よ asserts, か questions.
questions.explanation_hi: なあ वाक्य-अंत में मज़बूत व्यक्तिगत भावना ("सच में, है ना?")।

distractor_explanations.ね: ね seeks agreement / shared feeling at sentence end — not what's required here.
distractor_explanations.よ: よ emphasizes new information at sentence end — not what's required here.
distractor_explanations.か: か turns the sentence into a question — not what's required here.

distractor_explanations_hi.ね: ね वाक्यांत में सहमति / साझा भावना — यहाँ अपेक्षित नहीं।
distractor_explanations_hi.よ: よ वाक्यांत में नई जानकारी पर ज़ोर — यहाँ अपेक्षित नहीं।
distractor_explanations_hi.か: か वाक्य को प्रश्न बनाता है — यहाँ अपेक्षित नहीं।

questions.prompt_ja: （ ）に いちばん いい ものを えらんで ください。
questions.question_ja: あぶない！　そこへ 行く（ ）！
questions.explanation_en: V-plain (dictionary form) + な = strong/casual prohibition ('don't!'). The exclamatory あぶない！ context anchors the strong-register prohibition. Distractors: ね/よ are sentence-final tags but not prohibitions; の would form an informal question, not a command.
questions.explanation_hi: V-सादा (शब्दकोश) + な = मज़बूत/बोलचाल निषेध ("मत करो!")।

distractor_explanations.ね: ね seeks agreement / shared feeling at sentence end — not what's required here.
distractor_explanations.よ: よ emphasizes new information at sentence end — not what's required here.
distractor_explanations.の: の links nouns (possessive / attributive) or nominalizes — not what fits here.

distractor_explanations_hi.ね: ね वाक्यांत में सहमति / साझा भावना — यहाँ अपेक्षित नहीं।
distractor_explanations_hi.よ: よ वाक्यांत में नई जानकारी पर ज़ोर — यहाँ अपेक्षित नहीं।
distractor_explanations_hi.の: の संज्ञा-जोड़क (सम्बन्ध) या नामकरण — यहाँ नहीं फिट।

questions.prompt_ja: （ ）に いちばん いい ものを えらんで ください。
questions.question_ja: 「おなかが すきました。なに（ ）たべませんか。」
questions.explanation_en: なに + か = 'something' (positive existence). 'Want to eat something?' Distractors: なに + も + negative-verb = 'nothing' (would need ません), wo/ni don't form Q-word compounds.
questions.explanation_hi: なに + か = "कुछ" (सकारात्मक अस्तित्व)। "कुछ खाना चाहते हैं?"

distractor_explanations.を: を marks the direct object — not the role required here.
distractor_explanations.も: も means 'also / even' — not what fits here.
distractor_explanations.に: に marks time / destination / recipient — not the role required here.

distractor_explanations_hi.を: を प्रत्यक्ष कर्म चिह्नक — यहाँ अपेक्षित भूमिका नहीं।
distractor_explanations_hi.に: に समय / गंतव्य / पात्र चिह्नक — यहाँ अपेक्षित भूमिका नहीं।

questions.prompt_ja: （ ）に いちばん いい ものを えらんで ください。
questions.question_ja: つかれましたから、なに（ ）たべたく ありません。
questions.explanation_en: なに + も + negative = nothing. The negative たべたく ありません forces も (not か, which would be positive 'something'). Distractors: か is positive existence; を/が don't form Q-word compounds.
questions.explanation_hi: なに + も + नकार = "कुछ नहीं"।

distractor_explanations.が: が marks the grammatical subject (esp. with stative verbs / desire) — not what fits here.
distractor_explanations.か: か turns the sentence into a question — not what's required here.
distractor_explanations.を: を marks the direct object — not the role required here.

distractor_explanations_hi.が: が व्याकरणिक कर्ता चिह्नक (स्थिति-क्रिया / इच्छा के साथ) — यहाँ नहीं फिट।
distractor_explanations_hi.か: か वाक्य को प्रश्न बनाता है — यहाँ अपेक्षित नहीं।
distractor_explanations_hi.を: を प्रत्यक्ष कर्म चिह्नक — यहाँ अपेक्षित भूमिका नहीं।

questions.prompt_ja: （ ）に いちばん いい ものを えらんで ください。
questions.question_ja: へやに だれ（ ）いません。しずかです。
questions.explanation_en: だれ + も + negative-verb = no one. The negative いません forces も. Distractors: か is positive existence (would mean 'someone is there'); が/に don't form Q-word compounds.
questions.explanation_hi: だれ + も + नकारात्मक क्रिया = "कोई नहीं"।

distractor_explanations.か: か turns the sentence into a question — not what's required here.
distractor_explanations.が: が marks the grammatical subject (esp. with stative verbs / desire) — not what fits here.
distractor_explanations.に: に marks time / destination / recipient — not the role required here.

distractor_explanations_hi.か: か वाक्य को प्रश्न बनाता है — यहाँ अपेक्षित नहीं।
distractor_explanations_hi.が: が व्याकरणिक कर्ता चिह्नक (स्थिति-क्रिया / इच्छा के साथ) — यहाँ नहीं फिट।
distractor_explanations_hi.に: に समय / गंतव्य / पात्र चिह्नक — यहाँ अपेक्षित भूमिका नहीं।

questions.prompt_ja: （ ）に いちばん いい ものを えらんで ください。
questions.question_ja: つかれましたから、きょうは どこ（ ）行きません。
questions.explanation_en: どこ + も + negative-verb = nowhere. The negative 行きません forces も. Distractors: か is positive 'somewhere'; へ/を don't form Q-word compounds (they're plain particles).
questions.explanation_hi: どこ + も + नकारात्मक क्रिया = "कहीं नहीं"।

distractor_explanations.か: か turns the sentence into a question — not what's required here.
distractor_explanations.へ: へ marks direction toward — not what fits here.
distractor_explanations.を: を marks the direct object — not the role required here.

distractor_explanations_hi.か: か वाक्य को प्रश्न बनाता है — यहाँ अपेक्षित नहीं।
distractor_explanations_hi.へ: へ की-ओर दिशा-चिह्नक — यहाँ नहीं फिट।
distractor_explanations_hi.を: を प्रत्यक्ष कर्म चिह्नक — यहाँ अपेक्षित भूमिका नहीं।

questions.prompt_ja: （ ）に いちばん いい ものを えらんで ください。
questions.question_ja: わたしは いつ（ ）７じに おきます。
questions.explanation_en: いつ + も = always (positive existence; differs from なに/だれ/どこ which need a negative verb to form 'nothing/no-one/nowhere'). Distractors: いつ + か = 'sometime' (would need a non-habitual context); に/の don't form Q-word compounds.
questions.explanation_hi: いつ + も = "हमेशा" (सकारात्मक अस्तित्व — なに/だれ/どこ से अलग)।

distractor_explanations.に: に marks time / destination / recipient — not the role required here.
distractor_explanations.か: か turns the sentence into a question — not what's required here.
distractor_explanations.の: の links nouns (possessive / attributive) or nominalizes — not what fits here.

distractor_explanations_hi.に: に समय / गंतव्य / पात्र चिह्नक — यहाँ अपेक्षित भूमिका नहीं।
distractor_explanations_hi.か: か वाक्य को प्रश्न बनाता है — यहाँ अपेक्षित नहीं।
distractor_explanations_hi.の: の संज्ञा-जोड़क (सम्बन्ध) या नामकरण — यहाँ नहीं फिट।

questions.prompt_ja: （ ）に 入る ことばを えらんで ください。
questions.question_ja: 日本語を 話す こと（ ）できます。
questions.explanation_en: 「ことができる」 takes が on the こと nominalizer. Within the こと-clause the verb 話す retains its normal object 日本語を; only the outer こと takes が.

distractor_explanations.を: を marks 日本語 (the object of 話す) but not こと; こと is the subject of できる, which takes が.
distractor_explanations.に: に marks recipient/destination, not the subject of a できる-frame.
distractor_explanations.で: で marks means/location; here the structure specifies a verb noun-clause that is "doable", which takes が.

questions.explanation_hi: 「ことができる」 こと-नामकरणक पर が लेता है। अंदर 話す सामान्य कर्म 日本語を रखता है।

distractor_explanations_hi.を: を 日本語 (話す का कर्म) सूचित करता है, पर こと को नहीं; こと できる का कर्ता है, जो が लेता है।
distractor_explanations_hi.に: に प्राप्तकर्ता/गंतव्य सूचित करता है, できる-frame का कर्ता नहीं।
distractor_explanations_hi.で: で साधन/स्थान सूचित करता है; यहाँ संरचना एक क्रिया-संज्ञा-उपवाक्य निर्दिष्ट करती है जो "कर सकने योग्य" है, जो が लेता है।

questions.prompt_ja: （ ）に 入る ことばを えらんで ください。
questions.question_ja: あした 友だちに あう ことが （ ）。
questions.explanation_en: Verb-dict + ことが できません = "cannot do". The full negative form of the productive can-do construction.

distractor_explanations.ありません: ありません negates existence (ない). Pairing it with 「ことが」 yields 「ことが ありません」 = "have not done", a different N5 grammar (experience). Here we want capability negation.
distractor_explanations.いません: いません is the negative existence for animate subjects. Doesn't fit the こと frame.
distractor_explanations.しません: しません = "do not do" (volition / habit), not "cannot do". Loses the ability sense.

questions.explanation_hi: V-शब्दकोश + ことが できません = "नहीं कर सकता"। उत्पादक सकता है-करना का नकारात्मक।

distractor_explanations_hi.ありません: ありません अस्तित्व का नकारक (ない) है। 「ことが」 के साथ जोड़ने पर 「ことが ありません」 = "नहीं किया है" बनता है, जो अलग N5 व्याकरण (अनुभव) है। यहाँ क्षमता-नकार चाहिए।
distractor_explanations_hi.いません: いません सजीव कर्ताओं के लिए नकारात्मक अस्तित्व है। こと-frame से मेल नहीं खाता।
distractor_explanations_hi.しません: しません = "नहीं करता" (इच्छा/अभ्यास), "नहीं कर सकता" नहीं। क्षमता-भाव खो जाता है।
